actions.name: expired
actions.description: Look up all expired tokens.

actions.name: expunge_expired
actions.description: Deletes expired tokens.

actions.name: get_token
actions.description: Look up a token by JTI or token, and an optional purpose.

actions.name: revoke_all_stored_for_subject
actions.description: Revokes all stored tokens for a specific subject.

actions.name: revoke_jti
actions.description: Revoke a token by JTI. Creates a revocation token corresponding to the provided jti.

actions.name: revoke_token
actions.description: Revoke a token. Creates a revocation token corresponding to the provided token.

actions.name: revoked?
actions.description: Returns true if a revocation token is found for the provided token

actions.name: store_token
actions.description: Stores a token used for the provided purpose.

actions.name: create_with_password
actions.description: Create an operator sign-in account with a password.

actions.name: get_by_email
actions.description: Looks up a user by their email

actions.name: get_by_subject
actions.description: Get a user by the subject claim in a JWT

actions.name: request_password_reset_with_password
actions.description: Send password reset instructions to a user if they exist.

arguments.name: email
arguments.description: The proposed identity to send reset instructions to.

actions.name: set_password
actions.description: Set or rotate a user's password.

actions.name: sign_in_with_password
actions.description: Attempt to sign in using a username and password.

arguments.name: email
arguments.description: The identity to use for retrieving the user.

arguments.name: password
arguments.description: The password to check for the matching user.

actions.name: sign_in_with_remember_me
actions.description: Attempt to sign in using a remember me token.

arguments.name: token
arguments.description: The remember me token for authenticating

actions.name: sign_in_with_token
actions.description: Attempt to sign in using a short-lived sign in token.

domains.name: Acquisition
domains.description:
Unified intake layer for agent-discovered work.

This domain sits between the agent execution plane and downstream commercial
workflow. Procurement bids, discovery records, and future target-discovery
agents should all converge on durable acquisition findings before they become
commercial signals or pursuits.

resources.name: Document
resources.description:
Durable uploaded file record for acquisition intake.

The binary attachment lives here so a single document can be related to one
or many findings. Finding-specific context stays on `FindingDocument`.

resources.name: Finding
resources.description:
Unified raw intake record for agent-discovered work.

Findings are reviewed before they become active commercial opportunities.
This is the durable operator queue that procurement bids and discovery
targets now converge on.

resources.name: FindingDocument
resources.description:
Links an acquisition document to a finding with intake-specific metadata.

The document itself owns the uploaded file. This join resource keeps the
finding-side role and notes so the same document can be reused across
findings later without duplicating binaries.

resources.name: FindingReviewDecision
resources.description:
Durable operator review history for acquisition findings.

This keeps acceptance, rejection, suppression, parking, reopening, and
promotion rationale attached to the finding instead of scattering it across
origin metadata or transient UI state.

resources.name: Program
resources.description:
Operator-defined or agent-defined acquisition program.

Programs represent why the system is scanning, not where it scans. A program
might represent a market sweep, an industry watch, or a discovery lane.

attributes.name: context
attributes.description: Why this research is linked to this entity

resources.name: ResearchLink
resources.description:
Join resource linking ResearchRequests to any entity.

Enables a research request to be linked to multiple bids, organizations,
people, events, or discovery records. Each link carries optional
context explaining the connection.

attributes.name: researchable_id
attributes.description: ID of the entity to research

attributes.name: researchable_type
attributes.description: Type of entity: organization, person, bid, finding, discovery_record

resources.name: ResearchRequest
resources.description:
Research request for acquisition validation.

Tracks research tasks for organizations, people, bids, findings, and discovery records.
Uses AshStateMachine for status workflow.

relationships.name: assigned_team_member
relationships.description: Team member assigned to do the research

relationships.name: requested_by
relationships.description: User who requested the research

cross_domain_relationships.name: assigned_team_member
cross_domain_relationships.description: Team member assigned to do the research

cross_domain_relationships.name: requested_by
cross_domain_relationships.description: User who requested the research

resources.name: Source
resources.description: Durable registry of places the platform can scan for work.

domains.name: Agents
domains.description:
Agent control-plane domain.

Owns agent templates, deployments, runs, messages, and memory.

resources.name: Agent
resources.description:
Agent template definitions.

Stores metadata about available agent templates that can be spawned.

resources.name: AgentDeployment
resources.description:
Operator-managed configured agent deployments.

Deployments are the durable bridge between agent templates and runtime
executions. They hold ownership, visibility, schedule, and configuration.

resources.name: AgentMessage
resources.description:
Conversation history for agent runs.

Stores messages between user and agent, including tool calls and results.

resources.name: AgentRun
resources.description:
Execution tracking for agent runs.

Uses AshStateMachine to track the lifecycle of agent executions:
pending -> running -> completed/failed/cancelled

resources.name: AgentRunOutput
resources.description:
Durable business outputs produced by an `AgentRun`.

These records bridge runtime history to saved business entities like
`ProcurementSource`, `Bid`, and acquisition `Finding` without forcing the business records themselves to
track a single originating run forever.

resources.name: Memory
resources.description:
Persistent memory storage for agents.

Stores facts, patterns, decisions, and preferences that agents can
remember and recall across sessions.

actions.name: by_key
actions.description: Get a specific memory by its key

actions.name: by_type
actions.description: Find all memories of a specific type

actions.name: recall
actions.description: Search memories by query string matching key or content

actions.name: remember
actions.description: Store a memory with a key and content

actions.name: search
actions.description: Find all memories in a specific namespace

domains.name: Commercial
domains.description:
Commercial operating model domain.

Owns the business-development and agreement layer between market signals and
downstream delivery or service execution.

resources.name: Activity
resources.description:
Commercial activity resource.

Tracks interactions and touchpoints with organizations and people: calls,
emails, meetings, site visits, and demos.

relationships.name: bid
relationships.description: Related bid (if activity is about a bid)

relationships.name: organization
relationships.description: Related organization

relationships.name: owner_team_member
relationships.description: Team member who owns/performed this activity

relationships.name: person
relationships.description: Related person

relationships.name: pursuit
relationships.description: Related commercial pursuit

cross_domain_relationships.name: bid
cross_domain_relationships.description: Related bid (if activity is about a bid)

cross_domain_relationships.name: organization
cross_domain_relationships.description: Related organization

cross_domain_relationships.name: owner_team_member
cross_domain_relationships.description: Team member who owns/performed this activity

cross_domain_relationships.name: person
cross_domain_relationships.description: Related person

resources.name: Agreement
resources.description:
Signed or signable commercial agreement with a customer.

Agreements capture the commercial commitment that later drives projects,
work orders, invoicing, and contract consumption.

resources.name: ChangeOrder
resources.description:
Post-award commercial amendment attached to an agreement.

Change orders capture scope, schedule, or pricing deltas after award without
overwriting the original proposal or agreement intent.

resources.name: ChangeOrderLine
resources.description: Line item on a change order.

resources.name: CompanyProfile
resources.description:
Durable internal profile for how the company should describe and target itself.

This is the canonical business-facing source of truth for:

- positioning and specialty
- target industries and disqualifiers
- preferred language and tone guidance
- keyword modes that can later drive discovery and scoring

Agents and prompts can read from this profile, but the runtime layer should
not become the only place that knows what the company is.

resources.name: DiscoveryEvidence
resources.description:
Raw discovery evidence supporting a source discovery record.

Observations capture the exact evidence that caused a company to enter the
acquisition queue from a discovery program: hiring posts, expansion news,
legacy stack mentions, directory listings, or direct referrals.

resources.name: DiscoveryProgram
resources.description:
Durable definition of an outbound discovery or scouting motion.

Discovery programs describe where Gnome wants agents and operators to look
for new work. They scope the target industries, regions, search terms, and
watch channels that should produce discovery records and supporting evidence. The
program exists independently from the agent runtime so the commercial model
can answer which discovery motions are active and what they are producing.

resources.name: DiscoveryRecord
resources.description:
Source-specific discovery record that feeds the acquisition queue.

Discovery records are for programmatic outbound discovery where the system
needs a durable source record, evidence rollup, identity resolution, and
source-state transitions. Direct findings can enter Acquisition without this
wrapper and still be accepted or promoted once their own summary, source, and
work description are ready.

actions.name: create_opportunity
actions.description:
Create an opportunity discovery record (company seeking an integrator)
together with its backing Organization in a single transaction.

actions.name: create_prospect
actions.description:
Create a prospect discovery record together with its backing Organization
in a single transaction. Idempotent on website domain.

attributes.name: metadata
attributes.description: Extra context: score, workflow, source, etc.

attributes.name: subject_id
attributes.description: ID of the record this event is about

attributes.name: subject_type
attributes.description: Type of record: bid, finding, signal, pursuit

attributes.name: summary
attributes.description: Human-readable one-liner: 'Passed on SCADA bid — not in our area'

resources.name: Event
resources.description:
Append-only decision/audit log.

Records every meaningful state change across the commercial flow — passes,
pursues, stage advances, wins, losses — with who did it, why, and
what changed. Never edited or deleted.

Use for:
- Audit trail ("why did we pass on this?")
- Pattern analysis ("what do we keep passing on?")
- Team accountability ("who moved this forward?")

relationships.name: actor
relationships.description: Who made this decision

relationships.name: organization
relationships.description: Related organization (if exists)

cross_domain_relationships.name: actor
cross_domain_relationships.description: Who made this decision

cross_domain_relationships.name: organization
cross_domain_relationships.description: Related organization (if exists)

resources.name: Proposal
resources.description:
Commercial proposal or estimate prepared for a pursuit.

Proposals sit between internal pursuit qualification and signed agreements,
allowing Gnome to track issued quotes, revisions, acceptance, and expiry.

resources.name: ProposalLine
resources.description:
Line item on a commercial proposal.

Proposal lines capture the priced scope that ultimately rolls up into a
customer-facing quote or estimate.

resources.name: Pursuit
resources.description:
Internal revenue pursuit for a concrete business opportunity.

Pursuits turn qualified signals into forecastable pipeline and eventually
won or lost commercial outcomes.

resources.name: ServiceEntitlement
resources.description:
Included service capacity or allowance defined by an agreement.

Entitlements model what a contract includes, such as support labor, onsite
visits, inspections, or materials allowances, independent from how usage is
later recorded and billed.

resources.name: ServiceEntitlementUsage
resources.description:
Usage event recorded against a service entitlement.

This forms a contract-consumption ledger that can be driven manually today
and later automated from time entries, expenses, or work orders.

resources.name: ServiceLevelPolicy
resources.description:
Service response and resolution commitments attached to an agreement.

Policies can be applied to tickets manually or by future automation based on
severity, service type, or customer coverage terms.

resources.name: Signal
resources.description:
Raw business signal that may justify commercial follow-up.

Signals capture bids, inbound requests, promoted discovery findings,
referrals, renewal cues, or service-driven expansion opportunities before
Gnome commits to a formal pursuit.

attributes.name: related_to_type
attributes.description: Type of related record: organization, person, pursuit, bid

resources.name: Task
resources.description:
Commercial follow-up task resource.

Future to-dos and follow-up tasks related to commercial activities.
Can be linked to organizations, people, pursuits, or other records.

relationships.name: organization
relationships.description: Related organization

relationships.name: owner_team_member
relationships.description: Team member assigned to this task

relationships.name: person
relationships.description: Related person

relationships.name: pursuit
relationships.description: Related commercial pursuit

cross_domain_relationships.name: organization
cross_domain_relationships.description: Related organization

cross_domain_relationships.name: owner_team_member
cross_domain_relationships.description: Team member assigned to this task

cross_domain_relationships.name: person
cross_domain_relationships.description: Related person

domains.name: Execution
domains.description:
Execution operating model domain.

Owns project delivery and service execution records that fulfill commercial
agreements against organizations, sites, and managed systems.

resources.name: Assignment
resources.description:
Scheduled work allocation for project or service execution.

Assignments provide a unified dispatch and scheduling layer for digital work,
onsite visits, reviews, and coordinated execution across projects, work
items, and work orders.

resources.name: MaintenancePlan
resources.description: Preventive or recurring maintenance schedule attached to a managed asset.

resources.name: MaterialUsage
resources.description: Material, software, or license usage recorded against execution work.

resources.name: Project
resources.description:
Time-bound scoped delivery effort.

Projects represent finite implementation or upgrade work, distinct from
recurring service or maintenance work orders.

resources.name: ServiceTicket
resources.description:
Customer-facing service intake record.

Service tickets capture support, incident, warranty, and maintenance requests
before and alongside the work orders used to execute the actual work.

resources.name: WorkItem
resources.description:
Unified unit of project execution.

Work items model phases, milestones, deliverables, tasks, issues, and change
requests inside a project hierarchy.

resources.name: WorkOrder
resources.description:
Service or maintenance execution unit.

Work orders represent break/fix, inspection, commissioning, warranty, or
planned maintenance work that may stand alone or be tied to a project.

domains.name: Finance
domains.description:
Operational finance domain.

Owns billable and cost-bearing records that support project, service, and
agreement reporting without attempting to replace a full accounting ledger.

resources.name: Expense
resources.description: Non-labor cost incurred against customer or internal work.

resources.name: Invoice
resources.description:
Operational invoice header for billed work.

This is intentionally lightweight and designed to sync outward to a
dedicated accounting system if needed.

resources.name: InvoiceLine
resources.description:
Line item on an operational invoice.

Lines may reference labor, expense, service, or adjustment records so the
invoice header can be traced back to operational source data.

resources.name: Payment
resources.description: Operational record of received customer money.

resources.name: PaymentApplication
resources.description: Allocation of a payment to an invoice.

resources.name: TimeEntry
resources.description:
Time worked against an agreement, project, work item, or work order.

Time entries are the core operational-finance truth for labor cost and
billable utilization.

domains.name: Mercury
domains.description:
Mercury Bank domain.

Stores Mercury bank account and transaction data synced from the Mercury API
and webhooks. The PaymentMatch resource bridges Mercury transactions to
Finance.Payment records once the payment matcher runs.

resources.name: Account
resources.description:
A Mercury bank account belonging to the company.

Accounts are created during initial sync and updated by webhook events
when Mercury sends `balance.updated` notifications.

resources.name: ClientBankAlias
resources.description:
Maps a known wire/ACH counterparty name fragment to an Operations.Organization.

Populated automatically on the first confirmed payment match or manually via
AshAdmin. One organization can have multiple aliases to handle varying wire
counterparty name formats (e.g., "ACME CORP", "ACME CORPORATION").

resources.name: PaymentMatch
resources.description:
Links a Mercury bank transaction to a Finance.Payment record.

Created by MercuryPaymentMatcher (Oban job) with match_source: :auto,
or manually corrected with match_source: :manual.
A single Mercury transaction can match multiple Finance.Payment records
(e.g. one wire covering two invoices).

To undo a wrong match, destroy the record and create a correct one.

resources.name: Transaction
resources.description:
A Mercury bank transaction.

Transactions are inserted by the webhook receiver on `transaction.created`
events and updated on `transaction.updated` events. Status is owned by
Mercury — this resource only mirrors Mercury's state.

domains.name: Operations
domains.description:
Foundational operating model domain.

Owns the durable business entities that commercial work, delivery, service,
and finance hang off: organizations, people, sites, and managed systems.

resources.name: Asset
resources.description:
Installed or managed asset within a customer system.

Assets represent physical equipment, digital components, or hybrid elements
that benefit from service history and preventive maintenance planning.

resources.name: InventoryItem
resources.description:
Internal catalog or stocked item used in delivery and service work.

Inventory items cover hardware parts, consumables, software licenses, and
other billable or cost-bearing materials without attempting to model a full
warehouse system.

resources.name: ManagedSystem
resources.description:
A customer-facing system that Gnome sells, delivers, supports, or maintains.

Managed systems can be automation platforms, web applications, integrations,
or hybrid installations that combine physical and digital components.

resources.name: Organization
resources.description:
Durable record for an external or internal organization.

A single organization can play multiple roles over time, such as customer,
prospect, vendor, subcontractor, partner, or agency.

resources.name: OrganizationAffiliation
resources.description:
Connects an external person to an organization over a period of time.

Affiliations hold the business role a person plays for an organization,
such as buyer, billing contact, service contact, or technical stakeholder.

resources.name: Person
resources.description:
Durable external person record used across commercial work, service, and
delivery.

People are modeled independently from organizations so the same person can be
associated with multiple companies over time without duplicating identity.

resources.name: Site
resources.description:
A physical or digital operating location for an organization.

Sites can represent facilities, campuses, offices, cloud environments, labs,
or other delivery and service contexts.

resources.name: TeamMember
resources.description:
Durable operator profile for humans who can own, perform, or approve work.

Authentication users answer "who can sign in"; team members answer "who can
be assigned operational responsibility".

domains.name: Procurement
domains.description:
Procurement monitoring and opportunity discovery domain.

Owns monitored procurement sources and the bid opportunities discovered from
them. This is durable business state, not agent control-plane state.

attributes.name: score_company_profile_key
attributes.description: Company profile key used when the bid was scored

attributes.name: score_company_profile_mode
attributes.description: Company profile mode used when the bid was scored

attributes.name: score_geography
attributes.description: 0-20: SoCal = 20, NorCal = 12, Other CA = 8, Out of state = 0

attributes.name: score_icp_matches
attributes.description: Matched ICP lanes that explain why the bid fits

attributes.name: score_recommendation
attributes.description: Human-readable recommendation from the shared scoring model

attributes.name: score_risk_flags
attributes.description: Risk flags that explain why the bid may be weak or should be rejected

attributes.name: score_source_confidence
attributes.description: Confidence level for the source family that produced the bid

attributes.name: source_url
attributes.description: URL of the procurement source that found this

resources.name: Bid
resources.description:
Discovered bid/RFP opportunity.

Stores procurement opportunities found by the BidScanner agent,
including scoring based on service match, geography, value, and tech fit.

relationships.name: organization
relationships.description: Durable organization record for the issuing agency or customer

relationships.name: owner_team_member
relationships.description: Team member responsible for this bid

relationships.name: pursuits
relationships.description: Commercial pursuits created from this bid's signal

relationships.name: signal
relationships.description: Commercial signal created from this bid

cross_domain_relationships.name: organization
cross_domain_relationships.description: Durable organization record for the issuing agency or customer

cross_domain_relationships.name: owner_team_member
cross_domain_relationships.description: Team member responsible for this bid

cross_domain_relationships.name: pursuits
cross_domain_relationships.description: Commercial pursuits created from this bid's signal

cross_domain_relationships.name: signal
cross_domain_relationships.description: Commercial signal created from this bid

attributes.name: configured_at
attributes.description: When scrape config was saved or source was marked ready

attributes.name: metadata
attributes.description: Additional config: API keys, endpoints, etc.

attributes.name: portal_id
attributes.description: External portal ID (e.g., PlanetBids portal number)

attributes.name: scrape_config
attributes.description: CSS selectors for deterministic scanning (procurement sources)

attributes.name: scrape_selector
attributes.description: CSS selector for bid listings if scraping

resources.name: ProcurementSource
resources.description:
Universal monitored URL.

Every URL we check periodically — whether it's a government procurement
portal, a company website, a job board, or an industry directory. The
`source_type` determines which scanner strategy to use.

## Source Types & Scanners

| Type | Scanner | What it finds |
|------|---------|---------------|
| planetbids, opengov, bidnet, cal_eprocure, utility, school, port, custom | ListingScanner | Bids/RFPs |
| company_site | SiteScanner | Contacts, hiring signals, news |
| sam_gov | SAM.gov API (future) | Federal opportunities |
| job_board | JobScanner (future) | Hiring signals |
| directory | DirectoryScanner (future) | Company listings |

## State Machine

    found ──→ pending ──→ configured ──→ scanning (loops on cron)
                │              │              │
                ▼              ▼              ▼
           config_failed   scan_failed    (loops)
                │              │
                ▼              ▼
            pending        configured (retry)
                │
                ▼
             manual

Company sites can be created directly in `:configured` state since they
don't need CSS selector discovery.

actions.name: approve
actions.description: Approve this source for configuration and scanning

actions.name: block
actions.description: Block this source from future scanning

actions.name: configure
actions.description: Save discovered scraping configuration

actions.name: create_for_organization
actions.description: Create a pre-configured source for an organization website

actions.name: ignore
actions.description: Ignore this source without scanning it

actions.name: needs_configuration
actions.description: Sources needing scrape config discovery

actions.name: queue
actions.description: Queue source for SmartScanner configuration

actions.name: ready_for_scan
actions.description: Configured sources due for scanning

actions.name: reconsider
actions.description: Move this source back into the candidate pool

actions.name: scan
actions.description: Run scanner on this source (routed by source_type)

relationships.name: organization
relationships.description: Organization this source belongs to (for company_site type)

cross_domain_relationships.name: organization
cross_domain_relationships.description: Organization this source belongs to (for company_site type)

GnomeGarden.Agents.Memory.description:

Stores facts, patterns, decisions, and preferences that agents can
remember and recall across sessions.

## Source Types & Scanners

| planetbids, opengov, bidnet, cal_eprocure, utility, school, port, custom | ListingScanner | Bids/RFPs |

GnomeGarden.Execution.Assignment.name: Assignment
GnomeGarden.Execution.Assignment.description:
Scheduled work allocation for project or service execution.

Assignments provide a unified dispatch and scheduling layer for digital work,
onsite visits, reviews, and coordinated execution across projects, work
items, and work orders.

GnomeGarden.Acquisition.FindingDocument.name: FindingDocument
GnomeGarden.Acquisition.FindingDocument.description:
Links an acquisition document to a finding with intake-specific metadata.

The document itself owns the uploaded file. This join resource keeps the
finding-side role and notes so the same document can be reused across
findings later without duplicating binaries.

GnomeGarden.Mercury.Account.name: Account
GnomeGarden.Mercury.Account.description:
A Mercury bank account belonging to the company.

Accounts are created during initial sync and updated by webhook events
when Mercury sends `balance.updated` notifications.

GnomeGarden.Finance.TimeEntry.name: TimeEntry
GnomeGarden.Finance.TimeEntry.description:
Time worked against an agreement, project, work item, or work order.

Time entries are the core operational-finance truth for labor cost and
billable utilization.

GnomeGarden.Operations.TeamMember.name: TeamMember
GnomeGarden.Operations.TeamMember.description:
Durable operator profile for humans who can own, perform, or approve work.

Authentication users answer "who can sign in"; team members answer "who can
be assigned operational responsibility".

GnomeGarden.Execution.ServiceTicket.name: ServiceTicket
GnomeGarden.Execution.ServiceTicket.description:
Customer-facing service intake record.

Service tickets capture support, incident, warranty, and maintenance requests
before and alongside the work orders used to execute the actual work.

GnomeGarden.Finance.Invoice.name: Invoice
GnomeGarden.Finance.Invoice.description:
Operational invoice header for billed work.

This is intentionally lightweight and designed to sync outward to a
dedicated accounting system if needed.

GnomeGarden.Commercial.ProposalLine.name: ProposalLine
GnomeGarden.Commercial.ProposalLine.description:
Line item on a commercial proposal.

Proposal lines capture the priced scope that ultimately rolls up into a
customer-facing quote or estimate.

GnomeGarden.Mercury.PaymentMatch.name: PaymentMatch
GnomeGarden.Mercury.PaymentMatch.description:
Links a Mercury bank transaction to a Finance.Payment record.

Created by MercuryPaymentMatcher (Oban job) with match_source: :auto,
or manually corrected with match_source: :manual.
A single Mercury transaction can match multiple Finance.Payment records
(e.g. one wire covering two invoices).

To undo a wrong match, destroy the record and create a correct one.

GnomeGarden.Commercial.ChangeOrder.name: ChangeOrder
GnomeGarden.Commercial.ChangeOrder.description:
Post-award commercial amendment attached to an agreement.

Change orders capture scope, schedule, or pricing deltas after award without
overwriting the original proposal or agreement intent.

actions.name: expired
actions.description: Look up all expired tokens.

actions.name: expunge_expired
actions.description: Deletes expired tokens.

actions.name: get_token
actions.description: Look up a token by JTI or token, and an optional purpose.

actions.name: revoke_all_stored_for_subject
actions.description: Revokes all stored tokens for a specific subject.

actions.name: revoke_jti
actions.description: Revoke a token by JTI. Creates a revocation token corresponding to the provided jti.

actions.name: revoke_token
actions.description: Revoke a token. Creates a revocation token corresponding to the provided token.

actions.name: revoked?
actions.description: Returns true if a revocation token is found for the provided token

actions.name: store_token
actions.description: Stores a token used for the provided purpose.

GnomeGarden.Acquisition.Finding.name: Finding
GnomeGarden.Acquisition.Finding.description:
Unified raw intake record for agent-discovered work.

Findings are reviewed before they become active commercial opportunities.
This is the durable operator queue that procurement bids and discovery
targets now converge on.

GnomeGarden.Commercial.ServiceLevelPolic.name: ServiceLevelPolicy
GnomeGarden.Commercial.ServiceLevelPolic.description:
Service response and resolution commitments attached to an agreement.

Policies can be applied to tickets manually or by future automation based on
severity, service type, or customer coverage terms.

GnomeGarden.Execution.Project.name: Project
GnomeGarden.Execution.Project.description:
Time-bound scoped delivery effort.

Projects represent finite implementation or upgrade work, distinct from
recurring service or maintenance work orders.

GnomeGarden.Operations.Person.name: Person
GnomeGarden.Operations.Person.description:
Durable external person record used across commercial work, service, and
delivery.

People are modeled independently from organizations so the same person can be
associated with multiple companies over time without duplicating identity.

GnomeGarden.Operations.ManagedSystem.name: ManagedSystem
GnomeGarden.Operations.ManagedSystem.description:
A customer-facing system that Gnome sells, delivers, supports, or maintains.

Managed systems can be automation platforms, web applications, integrations,
or hybrid installations that combine physical and digital components.

GnomeGarden.Agents.Agent.name: Agent
GnomeGarden.Agents.Agent.description:
Agent template definitions.

Stores metadata about available agent templates that can be spawned.

GnomeGarden.Acquisition.FindingReviewDec.name: FindingReviewDecision
GnomeGarden.Acquisition.FindingReviewDec.description:
Durable operator review history for acquisition findings.

This keeps acceptance, rejection, suppression, parking, reopening, and
promotion rationale attached to the finding instead of scattering it across
origin metadata or transient UI state.

GnomeGarden.Commercial.DiscoveryRecord.name: DiscoveryRecord
GnomeGarden.Commercial.DiscoveryRecord.description:
Source-specific discovery record that feeds the acquisition queue.

Discovery records are for programmatic outbound discovery where the system
needs a durable source record, evidence rollup, identity resolution, and
source-state transitions. Direct findings can enter Acquisition without this
wrapper and still be accepted or promoted once their own summary, source, and
work description are ready.

actions.name: create_opportunity
actions.description:
Create an opportunity discovery record (company seeking an integrator)
together with its backing Organization in a single transaction.

actions.name: create_prospect
actions.description:
Create a prospect discovery record together with its backing Organization
in a single transaction. Idempotent on website domain.

GnomeGarden.Operations.Site.name: Site
GnomeGarden.Operations.Site.description:
A physical or digital operating location for an organization.

Sites can represent facilities, campuses, offices, cloud environments, labs,
or other delivery and service contexts.

GnomeGarden.Agents.AgentRunOutput.name: AgentRunOutput
GnomeGarden.Agents.AgentRunOutput.description:
Durable business outputs produced by an `AgentRun`.

These records bridge runtime history to saved business entities like
`ProcurementSource`, `Bid`, and acquisition `Finding` without forcing the business records themselves to
track a single originating run forever.

GnomeGarden.Finance.Expense.name: Expense
GnomeGarden.Finance.Expense.description: Non-labor cost incurred against customer or internal work.

attributes.name: score_company_profile_key
attributes.description: Company profile key used when the bid was scored

attributes.name: score_company_profile_mode
attributes.description: Company profile mode used when the bid was scored

attributes.name: score_geography
attributes.description: 0-20: SoCal = 20, NorCal = 12, Other CA = 8, Out of state = 0

attributes.name: score_icp_matches
attributes.description: Matched ICP lanes that explain why the bid fits

attributes.name: score_recommendation
attributes.description: Human-readable recommendation from the shared scoring model

attributes.name: score_risk_flags
attributes.description: Risk flags that explain why the bid may be weak or should be rejected

attributes.name: score_source_confidence
attributes.description: Confidence level for the source family that produced the bid

attributes.name: source_url
attributes.description: URL of the procurement source that found this

GnomeGarden.Procurement.Bid.name: Bid
GnomeGarden.Procurement.Bid.description:
Discovered bid/RFP opportunity.

Stores procurement opportunities found by the BidScanner agent,
including scoring based on service match, geography, value, and tech fit.

relationships.name: organization
relationships.description: Durable organization record for the issuing agency or customer

relationships.name: owner_team_member
relationships.description: Team member responsible for this bid

relationships.name: pursuits
relationships.description: Commercial pursuits created from this bid's signal

relationships.name: signal
relationships.description: Commercial signal created from this bid

cross_domain_relationships.name: organization
cross_domain_relationships.description: Durable organization record for the issuing agency or customer

cross_domain_relationships.name: owner_team_member
cross_domain_relationships.description: Team member responsible for this bid

cross_domain_relationships.name: pursuits
cross_domain_relationships.description: Commercial pursuits created from this bid's signal

cross_domain_relationships.name: signal
cross_domain_relationships.description: Commercial signal created from this bid

GnomeGarden.Commercial.Pursuit.name: Pursuit
GnomeGarden.Commercial.Pursuit.description:
Internal revenue pursuit for a concrete business opportunity.

Pursuits turn qualified signals into forecastable pipeline and eventually
won or lost commercial outcomes.

GnomeGarden.Acquisition.Document.name: Document
GnomeGarden.Acquisition.Document.description:
Durable uploaded file record for acquisition intake.

The binary attachment lives here so a single document can be related to one
or many findings. Finding-specific context stays on `FindingDocument`.

GnomeGarden.Operations.InventoryItem.name: InventoryItem
GnomeGarden.Operations.InventoryItem.description:
Internal catalog or stocked item used in delivery and service work.

Inventory items cover hardware parts, consumables, software licenses, and
other billable or cost-bearing materials without attempting to model a full
warehouse system.

GnomeGarden.Commercial.Signal.name: Signal
GnomeGarden.Commercial.Signal.description:
Raw business signal that may justify commercial follow-up.

Signals capture bids, inbound requests, promoted discovery findings,
referrals, renewal cues, or service-driven expansion opportunities before
Gnome commits to a formal pursuit.

GnomeGarden.Commercial.DiscoveryEvidence.name: DiscoveryEvidence
GnomeGarden.Commercial.DiscoveryEvidence.description:
Raw discovery evidence supporting a source discovery record.

Observations capture the exact evidence that caused a company to enter the
acquisition queue from a discovery program: hiring posts, expansion news,
legacy stack mentions, directory listings, or direct referrals.

attributes.name: researchable_id
attributes.description: ID of the entity to research

attributes.name: researchable_type
attributes.description: Type of entity: organization, person, bid, finding, discovery_record

GnomeGarden.Acquisition.ResearchRequest.name: ResearchRequest
GnomeGarden.Acquisition.ResearchRequest.description:
Research request for acquisition validation.

Tracks research tasks for organizations, people, bids, findings, and discovery records.
Uses AshStateMachine for status workflow.

relationships.name: assigned_team_member
relationships.description: Team member assigned to do the research

relationships.name: requested_by
relationships.description: User who requested the research

cross_domain_relationships.name: assigned_team_member
cross_domain_relationships.description: Team member assigned to do the research

cross_domain_relationships.name: requested_by
cross_domain_relationships.description: User who requested the research

GnomeGarden.Mercury.Transaction.name: Transaction
GnomeGarden.Mercury.Transaction.description:
A Mercury bank transaction.

Transactions are inserted by the webhook receiver on `transaction.created`
events and updated on `transaction.updated` events. Status is owned by
Mercury — this resource only mirrors Mercury's state.

attributes.name: related_to_type
attributes.description: Type of related record: organization, person, pursuit, bid

GnomeGarden.Commercial.Task.name: Task
GnomeGarden.Commercial.Task.description:
Commercial follow-up task resource.

Future to-dos and follow-up tasks related to commercial activities.
Can be linked to organizations, people, pursuits, or other records.

relationships.name: organization
relationships.description: Related organization

relationships.name: owner_team_member
relationships.description: Team member assigned to this task

relationships.name: person
relationships.description: Related person

relationships.name: pursuit
relationships.description: Related commercial pursuit

cross_domain_relationships.name: organization
cross_domain_relationships.description: Related organization

cross_domain_relationships.name: owner_team_member
cross_domain_relationships.description: Team member assigned to this task

cross_domain_relationships.name: person
cross_domain_relationships.description: Related person

GnomeGarden.Execution.WorkOrder.name: WorkOrder
GnomeGarden.Execution.WorkOrder.description:
Service or maintenance execution unit.

Work orders represent break/fix, inspection, commissioning, warranty, or
planned maintenance work that may stand alone or be tied to a project.

GnomeGarden.Operations.Asset.name: Asset
GnomeGarden.Operations.Asset.description:
Installed or managed asset within a customer system.

Assets represent physical equipment, digital components, or hybrid elements
that benefit from service history and preventive maintenance planning.

GnomeGarden.Commercial.Activity.name: Activity
GnomeGarden.Commercial.Activity.description:
Commercial activity resource.

Tracks interactions and touchpoints with organizations and people: calls,
emails, meetings, site visits, and demos.

relationships.name: bid
relationships.description: Related bid (if activity is about a bid)

relationships.name: organization
relationships.description: Related organization

relationships.name: owner_team_member
relationships.description: Team member who owns/performed this activity

relationships.name: person
relationships.description: Related person

relationships.name: pursuit
relationships.description: Related commercial pursuit

cross_domain_relationships.name: bid
cross_domain_relationships.description: Related bid (if activity is about a bid)

cross_domain_relationships.name: organization
cross_domain_relationships.description: Related organization

cross_domain_relationships.name: owner_team_member
cross_domain_relationships.description: Team member who owns/performed this activity

cross_domain_relationships.name: person
cross_domain_relationships.description: Related person

GnomeGarden.Mercury.ClientBankAlias.name: ClientBankAlias
GnomeGarden.Mercury.ClientBankAlias.description:
Maps a known wire/ACH counterparty name fragment to an Operations.Organization.

Populated automatically on the first confirmed payment match or manually via
AshAdmin. One organization can have multiple aliases to handle varying wire
counterparty name formats (e.g., "ACME CORP", "ACME CORPORATION").

GnomeGarden.Commercial.CompanyProfile.name: CompanyProfile
GnomeGarden.Commercial.CompanyProfile.description:
Durable internal profile for how the company should describe and target itself.

This is the canonical business-facing source of truth for:

- positioning and specialty
- target industries and disqualifiers
- preferred language and tone guidance
- keyword modes that can later drive discovery and scoring

Agents and prompts can read from this profile, but the runtime layer should
not become the only place that knows what the company is.

GnomeGarden.Commercial.Proposal.name: Proposal
GnomeGarden.Commercial.Proposal.description:
Commercial proposal or estimate prepared for a pursuit.

Proposals sit between internal pursuit qualification and signed agreements,
allowing Gnome to track issued quotes, revisions, acceptance, and expiry.

GnomeGarden.Commercial.Agreement.name: Agreement
GnomeGarden.Commercial.Agreement.description:
Signed or signable commercial agreement with a customer.

Agreements capture the commercial commitment that later drives projects,
work orders, invoicing, and contract consumption.

GnomeGarden.Operations.OrganizationAffil.name: OrganizationAffiliation
GnomeGarden.Operations.OrganizationAffil.description:
Connects an external person to an organization over a period of time.

Affiliations hold the business role a person plays for an organization,
such as buyer, billing contact, service contact, or technical stakeholder.

GnomeGarden.Execution.MaintenancePlan.name: MaintenancePlan
GnomeGarden.Execution.MaintenancePlan.description: Preventive or recurring maintenance schedule attached to a managed asset.

attributes.name: context
attributes.description: Why this research is linked to this entity

GnomeGarden.Acquisition.ResearchLink.name: ResearchLink
GnomeGarden.Acquisition.ResearchLink.description:
Join resource linking ResearchRequests to any entity.

Enables a research request to be linked to multiple bids, organizations,
people, events, or discovery records. Each link carries optional
context explaining the connection.

GnomeGarden.Execution.MaterialUsage.name: MaterialUsage
GnomeGarden.Execution.MaterialUsage.description: Material, software, or license usage recorded against execution work.

GnomeGarden.Commercial.ServiceEntitlemen.name: ServiceEntitlementUsage
GnomeGarden.Commercial.ServiceEntitlemen.description:
Usage event recorded against a service entitlement.

This forms a contract-consumption ledger that can be driven manually today
and later automated from time entries, expenses, or work orders.

GnomeGarden.Commercial.DiscoveryProgram.name: DiscoveryProgram
GnomeGarden.Commercial.DiscoveryProgram.description:
Durable definition of an outbound discovery or scouting motion.

Discovery programs describe where Gnome wants agents and operators to look
for new work. They scope the target industries, regions, search terms, and
watch channels that should produce discovery records and supporting evidence. The
program exists independently from the agent runtime so the commercial model
can answer which discovery motions are active and what they are producing.

GnomeGarden.Commercial.ServiceEntitlemen.name: ServiceEntitlement
GnomeGarden.Commercial.ServiceEntitlemen.description:
Included service capacity or allowance defined by an agreement.

Entitlements model what a contract includes, such as support labor, onsite
visits, inspections, or materials allowances, independent from how usage is
later recorded and billed.

GnomeGarden.Acquisition.Program.name: Program
GnomeGarden.Acquisition.Program.description:
Operator-defined or agent-defined acquisition program.

Programs represent why the system is scanning, not where it scans. A program
might represent a market sweep, an industry watch, or a discovery lane.

GnomeGarden.Operations.Organization.name: Organization
GnomeGarden.Operations.Organization.description:
Durable record for an external or internal organization.

A single organization can play multiple roles over time, such as customer,
prospect, vendor, subcontractor, partner, or agency.

GnomeGarden.Finance.Payment.name: Payment
GnomeGarden.Finance.Payment.description: Operational record of received customer money.

GnomeGarden.Finance.PaymentApplication.name: PaymentApplication
GnomeGarden.Finance.PaymentApplication.description: Allocation of a payment to an invoice.

GnomeGarden.Acquisition.Source.name: Source
GnomeGarden.Acquisition.Source.description: Durable registry of places the platform can scan for work.

GnomeGarden.Execution.WorkItem.name: WorkItem
GnomeGarden.Execution.WorkItem.description:
Unified unit of project execution.

Work items model phases, milestones, deliverables, tasks, issues, and change
requests inside a project hierarchy.

GnomeGarden.Commercial.ChangeOrderLine.name: ChangeOrderLine
GnomeGarden.Commercial.ChangeOrderLine.description: Line item on a change order.

attributes.name: metadata
attributes.description: Extra context: score, workflow, source, etc.

attributes.name: subject_id
attributes.description: ID of the record this event is about

attributes.name: subject_type
attributes.description: Type of record: bid, finding, signal, pursuit

attributes.name: summary
attributes.description: Human-readable one-liner: 'Passed on SCADA bid — not in our area'

GnomeGarden.Commercial.Event.name: Event
GnomeGarden.Commercial.Event.description:
Append-only decision/audit log.

Records every meaningful state change across the commercial flow — passes,
pursues, stage advances, wins, losses — with who did it, why, and
what changed. Never edited or deleted.

Use for:
- Audit trail ("why did we pass on this?")
- Pattern analysis ("what do we keep passing on?")
- Team accountability ("who moved this forward?")

relationships.name: actor
relationships.description: Who made this decision

relationships.name: organization
relationships.description: Related organization (if exists)

cross_domain_relationships.name: actor
cross_domain_relationships.description: Who made this decision

cross_domain_relationships.name: organization
cross_domain_relationships.description: Related organization (if exists)

GnomeGarden.Finance.InvoiceLine.name: InvoiceLine
GnomeGarden.Finance.InvoiceLine.description:
Line item on an operational invoice.

Lines may reference labor, expense, service, or adjustment records so the
invoice header can be traced back to operational source data.

GnomeGarden.Agents.AgentRun.name: AgentRun
GnomeGarden.Agents.AgentRun.description:
Execution tracking for agent runs.

Uses AshStateMachine to track the lifecycle of agent executions:
pending -> running -> completed/failed/cancelled
